task_name: FLS Training Equipment Overview - Multiple Tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, Intracorporeal Suture)
reason: Video is instructional/equipment demonstration content with multiple task setups shown sequentially, not a single continuous performance attempt. Includes product overview, equipment layout, and brief technique demonstrations but no timed, complete task execution from a trainee.
video_description: JDmeditech branded instructional video showing various FLS training tasks and equipment. Content includes: peg transfer setup with colored pegs and storage container, laparoscopic instruments (needle drivers, graspers), intracorporeal suture training model (pink foam), peg transfer practice with green spheres and blue cylinders, and endoloop/ligating loop demonstration on foam appendage model.

frames_visible.frame_description: Peg transfer board with 6 pegs and colored rubber objects in container
Laparoscopic instruments displayed: needle drivers, graspers, knot tying tools
Close-up of intracorporeal suture being placed through pink foam model with marked entry/exit points
frames_visible.task_indicated: Task 5: Intracorporeal Suture with Knot Tying

frames_visible.frame_description: Peg transfer task execution - green spheres being transferred between blue cylinders with graspers
Suture knot tying sequence shown on foam model with multiple throws visible
Penrose drain setup with ligating loop deployment visible (purple suture)
frames_visible.task_indicated: Task 3: Endoloop or Task 4: Extracorporeal Suture

demonstrated_techniques: Peg transfer setup and workspace organization
Proper laparoscopic instrument handling and positioning
Needle driver grasp position for suture placement
Suture entry and exit point marking on tissue models
Drain/appendage model manipulation during ligating loop deployment
teaching_content.content_purpose: Equipment demonstration and training setup overview for multiple FLS tasks

scoring_notes: This video does not contain a single, timed, complete trainee performance of any FLS task. It is educational content showing equipment, models, and brief technique demonstrations. Individual task execution is interrupted by scene changes, multiple task transitions, and no clear timer or completion end-point. Therefore, scoring using the FLS rubric is not appropriate.
recommendation: If scoring is required, submit individual performance videos showing: (1) Peg Transfer with full transfer sequence and documented time, (2) Pattern Cut with complete circular cut and time, (3) Endoloop with loop deployment and cinching, (4) Extracorporeal Suture with complete knot tying and time, or (5) Intracorporeal Suture with full surgeon's knot plus throws and documented completion time.
